content:
# Leviticus Chapter 2 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 58r–58v | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_002_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_002_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 86–87

---

**1** And when anyone brings a grain offering to the LORD, his offering shall be of fine flour. He shall pour oil on it and put frankincense on it,

**2** and he shall bring it to the sons of Aaron, the priests. And the priest shall take a handful of the fine flour and oil from it, along with all of its frankincense, and the priest shall burn this as its memorial portion on the altar, a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

**3** And what remains of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy portion from the fire offerings of the LORD.

**4** And if you bring a grain offering baked in the oven, it shall be unleavened loaves of fine flour mixed with oil, or unleavened wafers smeared with oil.

**5** And if your offering is a grain offering on a griddle, it shall be of fine flour, unleavened, mixed with oil.

**6** You shall break it in pieces and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.

**7** And if your offering is a grain offering in a pan, it shall be made of fine flour with oil.

**8** And you shall bring the grain offering that is made from these things to the LORD. And it shall be presented to the priest, and he shall bring it to the altar.

**9** And the priest shall lift out from the grain offering its memorial portion, and shall burn it on the altar, a fire offering, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

**10** And what remains of the grain offering shall belong to Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy portion from the fire offerings of the LORD.

**11** No grain offering that you bring to the LORD shall be made with leaven, for you shall burn no leaven and no honey as a fire offering to the LORD.

**12** As an offering of firstfruits you may bring them to the LORD, but they shall not go up on the altar for a pleasing aroma.

**13** And every grain offering of yours you shall season with salt. You shall not let the salt of the covenant of your God be missing from your grain offering; with all your offerings you shall offer salt.

**14** And if you bring a grain offering of firstfruits to the LORD, you shall bring your firstfruits grain offering as fresh ears roasted with fire, crushed new grain.

**15** And you shall put oil on it and lay frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.

**16** And the priest shall burn as its memorial portion some of the crushed grain and some of the oil along with all its frankincense, a fire offering to the LORD.

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

**Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 58r–58v.** Both pages of the Leningrad Codex were examined directly. The text is written in the characteristic three-column layout of the B19A. Leviticus 2 opens on folio 58r, following immediately from the close of chapter 1. The chapter's compact legal structure — organizing the grain offering laws by mode of preparation (raw flour, oven-baked, griddle-cooked, pan-cooked, and firstfruits) — is clearly visible in the Masorah-annotated columns. The Masorah parva notations appear in the margins, with several annotations pointing to the repeated occurrences of minchah and azkarah throughout the chapter. Folio 58v continues with the remainder of the chapter through verse 16.

The key crux in verse 14 — אָבִיב קָלוּי בָּאֵשׁ גֶּרֶשׂ כַּרְמֶל — is legible in the manuscript: aviv ("fresh ears / green grain"), qaluy ba-esh ("roasted in fire"), geres ("crushed"), karmel ("new grain / carmel"). This hapax-rich construction is fully preserved in translation.

**Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 86–87 (LXX).** Pages 86 and 87 of the Vaticanus were examined. Page 86 (pa_0086) contains the end of the Leviticus 1 material and the opening of chapter 2, and page 87 (pa_0087) contains the continuation into chapter 3 material ("CAP. XXXI" visible at the top right of the second Vaticanus image). The LXX of Leviticus 2 is labeled as part of the uncial continuous text. The Greek renders the chapter generally in close agreement with the MT, with some significant variations noted below.

---

### Key Translation Decisions

1. **Minchah / "grain offering" (throughout).** Hebrew מִנְחָה (minchah) has a broad semantic range: it can mean "gift," "tribute," or "offering," depending on context. In Genesis, it is Jacob's "gift" to Esau (Gen 32:14). In the cultic legislation of Leviticus, it has narrowed to the specific non-animal cereal/grain offering. "Grain offering" is the standard formal equivalence rendering (ESV, NASB) and is adopted throughout this chapter. The LXX renders it θυσία ("sacrifice") or σεμίδαλις ("fine flour offering," when the material itself is specified), reflecting both the offering type and its primary ingredient.

2. **Azkarah / "memorial portion" (vv. 2, 9, 16).** Hebrew אַזְכָּרָה (azkarah) is a technical term for the portion of the grain offering that is burned on the altar as a representative memorial before the LORD. It derives from the root זכר (zakar, "to remember"), and carries the sense of the offering "bringing to remembrance" before God. "Memorial portion" follows the majority of formal equivalence translations and is more precise than "token portion" (NRSV) or "representative portion." The LXX renders it μνημόσυνον ("memorial, remembrance"), which confirms the semantic derivation and supports this rendering.

3. **"Handful" (v. 2).** Hebrew קֹמֶץ (qomets) — literally "a cupped handful" — is the precise portion the priest takes. This translation renders it "a handful" rather than "a fistful" or "as much as his hand can hold," following established liturgical usage. The LXX has δράκα ("a handful, a fist-grasp"), which confirms the sense.

4. **Levonah / "frankincense" (vv. 1, 2, 15, 16).** Hebrew לְבֹנָה (levonah) is the aromatic resin offering that accompanied the grain offering. "Frankincense" is the standard English rendering of this term. The LXX uses λίβανος ("frankincense"), in direct agreement.

5. **"Mixed with oil" vs. "smeared with oil" (v. 4).** Verse 4 presents two forms of oven-baked grain offering: (a) loaves (חַלּוֹת, challot) "mixed with oil" (בְּלוּלֹת בַּשֶּׁמֶן, belulot ba-shemen) and (b) wafers (רְקִיקֵי, reqiqi) "smeared with oil" (מְשֻׁחִים בַּשֶּׁמֶן, meshuchim ba-shemen). The distinction is deliberate and preserved: the loaves have oil incorporated throughout the dough, while the wafers are anointed on the surface. "Mixed" and "smeared" capture this culinary-cultic difference precisely. The LXX uses πεφυραμένους ἐν ἐλαίῳ ("kneaded with oil") and κεχρισμένους ἐν ἐλαίῳ ("anointed/smeared with oil"), fully supporting this distinction.

6. **Chametz / "leaven" and Devash / "honey" (v. 11).** Hebrew שְׂאֹר (se'or, "leaven/sourdough") and דְּבַשׁ (devash, "honey") are both prohibited as fire offerings to the LORD. "Leaven" is unambiguous; "honey" is the standard rendering of devash, though scholars debate whether it includes date-syrup (dibs) in addition to bee honey. The translation renders devash as "honey" without qualification, following the plain lexical meaning. The reason for the prohibition is not stated in the text; the translation does not supply one. The LXX renders ζύμη ("leavened dough") and μέλι ("honey"), in agreement with MT.

7. **"Offering of firstfruits" / Reshit (v. 12).** Hebrew רֵאשִׁית (reshit, "firstfruits / first portion") is rendered "offering of firstfruits," maintaining the agricultural-covenantal context. The verse grants an exception: the firstfruits may be brought to the LORD but may not ascend on the altar for a pleasing aroma. This preserves the distinction between what is "brought to" (offered in the sanctuary) and what "goes up on" (is burned on the altar). The LXX renders ἀπαρχή ("firstfruits"), in agreement.

8. **Melach berit Elohekha / "the salt of the covenant of your God" (v. 13).** This is one of the most theologically weighted phrases in the chapter. Hebrew מֶלַח בְּרִית אֱלֹהֶיךָ (melach berit Elohekha) — "the salt of the covenant of your God" — is translated in full, preserving the construct chain. Salt is a symbol of preservation, permanence, and covenant loyalty in the ancient Near East (cf. Num 18:19, 2 Chr 13:5, "covenant of salt"). The command is total: salt shall not be absent from any offering. The translation does not explain the metaphor; it presents the text. The LXX renders ἅλας διαθήκης Κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ σου ("salt of the covenant of the LORD your God"), adding Κυρίου before θεοῦ σου — a characteristic LXX expansion.

9. **"Fresh ears roasted with fire, crushed new grain" (v. 14).** Hebrew אָבִיב קָלוּי בָּאֵשׁ גֶּרֶשׂ כַּרְמֶל is a dense, hapax-rich construction. The rendering follows the best-attested understanding:
   - aviv: "fresh ears" (green/fresh grain still on the stalk; cf. Exod 9:31)
   - qaluy ba-esh: "roasted with fire" (parched grain; cf. Ruth 2:14)
   - geres: "crushed" (ground, dehusked grain)
   - karmel: "new grain / carmel" (a term for fresh, ripe grain; LXX renders it νέα "new")
   The translation "fresh ears roasted with fire, crushed new grain" attempts to preserve each element without conflating them. This is the grain in its most primitive and natural state — still on the stalk, fire-roasted, and then crushed — contrasting with the refined fine flour (solet) of vv. 1-10.

10. **"Most holy portion" (vv. 3, 10).** Hebrew קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים (qodesh qodashim, "holy of holies / most holy") applied to Aaron's priestly portion of the grain offering is rendered "most holy portion." The superlative construct is preserved. The LXX renders ἅγιον τῶν ἁγίων ("holy of holies"), in direct agreement.

---

### LXX Vaticanus Variants (pp. 86–87) — Significant Divergences from MT

| Verse | MT (Leningrad Codex) | LXX (Vaticanus) | Note |
|---|---|---|---|
| 2:1 | minchah ("grain offering") | θυσία / σεμίδαλις ("sacrifice" / "fine flour offering") | LXX varies: sometimes renders minchah as θυσία (general sacrifice), sometimes specifies σεμίδαλις (fine flour). The MT's specific minchah terminology is more precise. |
| 2:2 | azkarah ("memorial portion") | μνημόσυνον ("memorial / remembrance") | LXX confirms the derivation from zakar; direct lexical agreement. |
| 2:4 | "loaves mixed with oil / wafers smeared with oil" (belulot / meshuchim) | πεφυραμένους ἐν ἐλαίῳ / κεχρισμένους ἐν ἐλαίῳ ("kneaded" / "anointed") | LXX preserves the same distinction between the two preparation methods; fully supports the MT's nuanced dough vs. surface-application distinction. |
| 2:11 | se'or / devash ("leaven" / "honey") | ζύμη / μέλι | LXX agrees exactly with MT on both prohibitions. |
| 2:12 | "they shall not go up on the altar for a pleasing aroma" | οὐκ ἀνενεχθήσεται ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον εἰς ὀσμὴν εὐωδίας | LXX agrees closely; confirms the firstfruits exception for altar-burning. |
| 2:13 | melach berit Elohekha ("the salt of the covenant of your God") | ἅλας διαθήκης Κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ σου ("salt of the covenant of the LORD your God") | LXX adds Κυρίου (Lord) before θεοῦ σου, a characteristic LXX expansion. This translation follows the shorter MT. |
| 2:14 | aviv qaluy ba-esh geres karmel | χιδρᾶ πεφρυγμένα ("roasted/parched grains") with νέα ("new grain") | LXX condenses the four-term construct into fewer terms; χιδρά (parched grain) covers both aviv and qaluy ba-esh. The translation follows the fuller MT. |
| 2:16 | "memorial portion of crushed grain and oil" | μνημόσυνον ἀπὸ τῶν χιδρῶν ("memorial from the parched grains") | LXX agrees in function; minor condensation of the MT's double-reference to geres and shemen. |

---

### Consistent Term Renderings (Leviticus Series — Updated with Chapter 2)

| Hebrew | LOGOS Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| מִנְחָה (minchah) | grain offering | Cereal/grain tribute-offering; distinct from animal burnt offering |
| אַזְכָּרָה (azkarah) | memorial portion | Portion burned on altar as memorial before the LORD |
| לְבֹנָה (levonah) | frankincense | Aromatic resin accompanying the grain offering |
| קֹמֶץ (qomets) | handful | Precise technical term for the priestly portion taken |
| שְׂאֹר (se'or) | leaven | Prohibited from fire offerings |
| דְּבַשׁ (devash) | honey | Prohibited from fire offerings |
| מֶלַח בְּרִית (melach berit) | salt of the covenant | Full construct preserved; covenant permanence symbol |
| אִשֶּׁה (isheh) | fire offering | Retained from Lev 1; distinct technical term |
| רֵיחַ נִיחֹחַ (re'ach nichoach) | pleasing aroma | Anthropomorphic idiom; consistent throughout |
| יהוה | the LORD | YHWH — consistent throughout |
| אֹהֶל מוֹעֵד | tent of meeting | Established convention |
| קֹדֶשׁ קָדָשִׁים (qodesh qodashim) | most holy portion | Superlative construct; Aaron's priestly share |

— LOGOS
